generated.html:
Hey,
me Lukas

now. Programmer from Germany. This is my cool, really important programmer portfolio. I hope that this torment with CSS was worth it


OSS Contributions Personal OSS Projects CV LinkedIn Twitter

If you have a cool problem, or want to talk to me then message me here

What I like to spend my time with (fine not all of my time goes into those things):

A cool problem, Programming, Learning something new, Technology, Bikes, Enduro, Nature (this is biggg)

What I dislike and not spend my time with (ok just a little bit):

frontend development (CSS primarily, also a lot of the JS ecos is really confusing and just bad), scammers, social credit seekers, energy eaters, ads, news, drama, bureaucracy, marketing bullshit

like 95% of my stuff is done with (that is indeed true):

Go(I just love golang), C++(Ok not really), Rust(::ohyea), Python(slooow. you slooww) or Nim (is also pretty cool)

Will focus on compiled langs in the future. Think it's just a better concept.

Usually writing low level stuff with Rust or Nim, servers, automation and tooling with Go and data heavy projects like ETL processes with Python

Tools I really really like and use daily (most):

Git, Vim, Brave Browser, Docker, Bitwarden, MacBook Pro, Arch Linux, tmux, Flask, Pandas, NumPy, Parcel(makes js bundling easier, good direction for js ecos), WASD/CODE Keyboards

What type of developer am I?

When I design product I:

... usually try to create a decentralized version instead of a centralized version. An anonymous version indstead of a user login. Lesser amazing features instead of a lot messed up features.

... think of a problem to be solved in the easiest possible way. I do things rather fast and proactive, and yes sure it takes longer that way, but it also makes me happy to learn that way. (Big into learning. love that)

Important:

When it becomes work and does not remain learning, it becomes creeping, pressing, bland, unwanted.



I value privacy and self will so anything you do here is secure. I dont log anything, run analytics or track you.

lukasjoc,







Nice to watch

Free to Choose, Der Alchimist, Siddhartha, Demian, Stunden im Garten , Platon und Nietzsche Un-Zeitgenossen im Gespräch, Traue keinem Scan, den du nicht selbst gefälscht hast, SpiegelMining, BahnMining, ROHDATEN sind soo geil... 🐼🐼, become your own alchemist, guided meditation by sadhguru, Free Programming Books, Transforming Code into Beautiful, Idiomatic Python, Writing Beautiful Packages in Go, 10 Tips For Clean Code,

Great books and reading

The Hacker Manifesto, A Casino Odyssey in Cyberspace, Crime and Punishment, The Metamorphosis of Prime Intellect, The Secret, Infinite Jest, Atlas Shrugged, Permutation City, Vorlesungen zur Einführung in die Psychoanalyse,
